journal_entry.description: Client entertainment meal paid with corporate credit card
journal_entry.rationale: Client entertainment meals are recorded as Meals and Entertainment Expense (or similar expense account). The corporate credit card creates a liability (Credit Card Payable) that will be settled later. Under Canadian tax rules, only 50% of meals and entertainment expenses are deductible for tax purposes, but the full amount is recorded as an expense with the limitation applied at tax filing. No HST/GST is recoverable on entertainment expenses per CRA rules.

output.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (accounts payable/credit card payable increases when a purchase is made on credit)

credit_classifier.reason: Corporate credit card used = liability increase (accounts payable/credit card payable increases when a purchase is made on credit)

output.reason: Client entertainment meal = expense increase. The $125 meal is a business expense (entertainment/meals and entertainment expense). The debit increases the expense account. The credit side (not classified here) would increase the liability (corporate credit card payable).
